User story: As a new user I want a clear explanation before system permission prompts so I understand why the app asks for sensitive access.
Expected behavior based on code: First-time launch enters permission explanation before requesting permissions; the screen lists categorized permissions using resource-backed descriptions and continues into permission requests.

User story: As a user I want Bluetooth, location, optional notification, and conditionally Wi-Fi Aware permissions requested only as needed so setup can complete without manual restart.
Expected behavior based on code: OnboardingCoordinator requests missing required permissions plus optional notification permission, checks critical required grants, routes to background location, completion, partial-warning completion, or error. Microphone is not part of onboarding.

User story: As a user I want the app to detect disabled or unsupported Bluetooth and guide me to enable it so mesh chat can work.
Expected behavior based on code: Existing users are checked before app initialization unless BLE is disabled in debug settings; disabled or unsupported Bluetooth shows BluetoothCheckScreen; skip is session-scoped.
Notes: Requires hardware or emulator capability checks.

User story: As a user I want the app to detect disabled or unavailable location services because Android BLE scanning depends on location state.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationCheckScreen appears when services are disabled or unavailable; enabled services allow onboarding to continue to battery optimization or permissions.

User story: As a user I want to decide whether to grant background location so mesh reliability can improve while I understand it is optional.
Expected behavior based on code: If Android version needs background location and it is not granted or skipped, the flow shows BackgroundLocationPermissionScreen with continue, retry, and skip paths.

User story: As a user I want the app to explain battery optimization tradeoffs and let me disable or skip it so background mesh is reliable.
Expected behavior based on code: BatteryOptimizationScreen appears for existing users when optimization is enabled and not skipped; disable, retry, and skip paths are wired. First-time launch skips this check until after permissions.

User story: As a user I want failed startup to show a recoverable error with retry and settings actions.
Expected behavior based on code: Initialization verifies permissions, starts unified mesh services, handles notification/verification intents, and updates MainViewModel to COMPLETE. Failures update error state and show InitializationErrorScreen with retry and app settings actions; error strings are resource-backed.
Notes: Can be unit tested with state transitions.

User story: As a returning user I want the app to notice if Bluetooth or location was disabled in the background so messaging does not silently fail.
Expected behavior based on code: onResume reattaches the mesh delegate when onboarding is complete, rechecks Bluetooth and location, routes back to the relevant check screen if disabled, and starts Wi-Fi Aware when location is enabled.

User story: As a user I want the mesh to keep running in the background with a persistent notification showing peer count.
Expected behavior based on code: MeshForegroundService.start only launches when background mode, Bluetooth/location, and notification permission gates allow it; onStartCommand creates or reuses mesh services, starts Wi-Fi Aware/BLE as eligible, promotes to foreground, updates peer count every 5 seconds, and clears the notification when eligibility is lost.

User story: As a user I want a notification action to fully quit bitchat so background network activity stops.
Expected behavior based on code: Notification action sends ACTION_QUIT; service cancels update work, stops foreground state, clears notification, stops mesh and Tor best-effort through AppShutdownCoordinator, clears AppStateStore, broadcasts UI force-finish, and kills the process after shutdown timeout.

User story: As a user who enabled background operation I want the app to resume mesh after reboot when permissions allow.
Expected behavior based on code: AndroidManifest declares RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETED and registers BootCompletedReceiver for BOOT_COMPLETED and LOCKED_BOOT_COMPLETED. BootCompletedReceiver initializes MeshServicePreferences and starts MeshForegroundService only when auto-start is enabled.
Notes: Runtime reboot behavior still needs device/instrumented verification.

User story: As a user I want a nickname assigned automatically and editable from the header so peers see a human-readable name.
Expected behavior based on code: DataManager loads saved nickname or creates anon####; NicknameEditor edits header text; ChatViewModel.setNickname updates state, persists the value, and sends a broadcast announce.

User story: As a user I want to type and send a public mesh message so nearby peers can receive it.
Expected behavior based on code: Chat input sends trimmed non-empty text; ChatViewModel routes commands first, then sends private, geohash, channel, encrypted-channel, or public mesh messages; public mesh sends are locally added through MessageManager/AppStateStore before mesh send.

User story: As a user I want incoming mesh messages to appear once in the timeline with sender metadata.
Expected behavior based on code: Bluetooth and Wi-Fi Aware receive paths add non-channel messages to AppStateStore with id/content de-duplication; ChatViewModel collects publicMessages into ChatState; MeshDelegateHandler separately deduplicates callback handling for notifications and blocked-sender checks.

User story: As a user I want text, media, sender, timestamp, mentions, channels, and status rendered consistently in chat.
Expected behavior based on code: ChatScreen selects the active public, classic channel, or geohash timeline; MessagesList reverse-renders latest messages; MessageItem delegates image/audio/file rendering, formats text with sender/timestamp/mentions/geohash/URL annotations, and overlays delivery status for own private messages.

User story: As a user reading older messages I want a visible control to jump back to the newest message.
Expected behavior based on code: MessagesList tracks whether the reverse-layout list is near index 0, reports scrolled-up state, follows incoming messages only while at latest, and ChatScreen shows an ArrowDownward IconButton that toggles forced scroll to item 0.

User story: As a user I want tapping a sender nickname to insert an @mention into the input.
Expected behavior based on code: Message text/header add nickname_click annotations for non-self senders; ChatScreen inserts @nickname with spacing and preserves geohash hash suffixes when the selected location channel is active.

User story: As a user I want long-pressing a message to open actions for the message and user.
Expected behavior based on code: Message text and media/header long-press callbacks pass the selected BitchatMessage to ChatScreen; ChatScreen stores the base sender and selected message, then presents ChatUserSheet with copy, private message, slap, hug, and block actions as applicable.

User story: As a user I want to copy message text from the action sheet.
Expected behavior based on code: ChatUserSheet shows Copy Message when selectedMessage exists and writes selectedMessage.content to LocalClipboardManager before dismissing.
Notes: Needs clipboard instrumentation; media/file messages may copy their content/path representation rather than a human-friendly summary.

User story: As a user in an emergency I want triple-tapping the app title to clear messages, keys, settings, media, notifications, and restart identity.
Expected behavior based on code: MainHeader wires the app brand to singleOrTripleClickable; triple-click calls panicClearAllData, which clears message/channel/private chat/data managers, seen message store, mesh data, cryptographic data, notifications, media, geohash/bookmark state, resets nickname, recreates MeshServiceHolder services, restarts mesh, and broadcasts announce.
Notes: High-risk; requires dedicated device/instrumented test plan.

User story: As a user I want app identity, version, feature summaries, and settings in one sheet.
Expected behavior based on code: Tapping the app title opens AboutSheet; it reads app version, renders identity/tagline, feature rows, theme/background/PoW/Tor controls, emergency warning, optional debug settings link, footer, and close action. Several hardcoded AboutSheet setting/status labels were moved to string resources during inspection.
Errors documented: Localized several hardcoded AboutSheet labels/status strings found during static inspection.
Notes: Needs UI screenshot and localization smoke test.

User story: As a user I want system, light, or dark theme selection persisted across launches.
Expected behavior based on code: AboutSheet theme chips collect ThemePreferenceManager.themeFlow and write System, Light, or Dark to SharedPreferences; BitchatApplication initializes the manager and BitchatTheme consumes the flow to choose system, light, or dark Material color schemes.

User story: As a user I want to enable or disable background mesh operation from settings.
Expected behavior based on code: AboutSheet background toggle mirrors MeshServicePreferences.isBackgroundEnabled, persists changes, stops MeshForegroundService when disabled, and starts it when re-enabled. MeshForegroundService still gates foreground start on Bluetooth/location and notification permissions.

User story: As a user I want to route internet traffic over Tor when available and see bootstrap status.
Expected behavior based on code: AboutSheet disables the Tor toggle when ArtiTorManager reports unavailable, persists ON/OFF through TorPreferenceManager when available, shows status color and bootstrap/log detail while ON, and ChatHeader shows a compact Tor status dot when mode is not OFF.
Errors documented: Localized hardcoded AboutSheet Tor title and connected/disconnected status labels during static inspection.

User story: As a user I want optional PoW for geohash messages to reduce spam.
Expected behavior based on code: AboutSheet initializes PoWPreferenceManager, toggles enabled state, shows a 0-32 difficulty slider, and displays mining estimate; GeohashViewModel marks local geohash messages with configured difficulty, NostrProtocol mines events before signing when enabled, and NostrClient/GeohashMessageHandler reject insufficient-PoW incoming events.
Code evidence: AboutSheet.kt; PoWPreferenceManager.kt; PoWStatusIndicator.kt; GeohashViewModel.kt; NostrProtocol.kt; NostrClient.kt; GeohashMessageHandler.kt; NostrProofOfWork.kt
Errors documented: Localized hardcoded AboutSheet PoW section/value labels during static inspection.

User story: As a developer/user I want diagnostics for logging, BLE roles, transports, packet relay, sync, devices, scans, and console output.
Expected behavior based on code: AboutSheet opens DebugSettingsSheet through onShowDebug; DebugSettingsSheet collects DebugSettingsManager flows for verbose logging, BLE/GATT toggles and limits, Wi-Fi Aware toggles/support, packet relay stats/graphs, sync settings, connected device actions, and console messages; DebugPreferenceManager persists preference-backed toggles.
Errors documented: None found in this static inspection; broader localization/debug copy caveat remains in F089.
Notes: Large UI surface; needs focused smoke tests.

User story: As a user I want a compact network count and a detailed sheet of peers, channels, and network state.
Expected behavior based on code: Header PeerCounter displays mesh connected peer count or geohash participant count plus joined channel count; tapping it calls onSidebarClick and opens MeshPeerListSheet, which lists joined channels, mesh peers, offline favorites, unread DM indicators, transport/status icons, verification state, and geohash people when in a location channel.

Feature: Mesh peer list private chat launch
User story: As a user I want to tap a peer and open a private chat sheet.
Expected behavior based on code: MeshPeerListSheet peer rows call onPrivateChatStart, which sets privateChatSheetPeer and dismisses the network sheet; ChatScreen renders PrivateChatSheet, and PrivateChatSheet starts the private chat in LaunchedEffect(peerID), initializing state, clearing unread markers, and starting Noise handshake if needed.

User story: As a user I want to send direct encrypted messages to a peer.
Expected behavior based on code: PrivateChatManager starts chats only when not blocked, initiates or prompts Noise session setup, creates a local private BitchatMessage with Sending status, stores it in the peer conversation/AppStateStore, and ChatViewModel routes send through MessageRouter, which sends over ready mesh, sends over Nostr for mapped mutual favorites/geohash aliases, queues otherwise, and initiates handshake when mesh-connected but not ready.
Notes: Needs fake MeshService and router test.

Feature: Private message receive and unread state
User story: As a user I want incoming DMs to appear in the peer conversation and mark unread until viewed.
Expected behavior based on code: Mesh-origin private messages are stored by transport/AppStateStore and collected into ChatState; PrivateChatManager initializes the conversation, blocks blocked senders, adds Nostr-origin messages directly, marks conversations unread when not focused, sends read receipts and clears unread on focus, and ChatViewModel persists read IDs in SeenMessageStore so recreation does not re-mark read messages unread.

User story: As a user I want private messages to show sending, sent, delivered, failed, and read states.
Expected behavior based on code: PrivateChatManager creates outgoing private messages with Sending status, now marks them Sent after the router callback accepts the send and Failed if that callback throws; MessageManager applies priority-based status updates and syncs AppStateStore; MeshDelegateHandler maps delivery acks to Delivered and read receipts to Read; MessageComponents renders all DeliveryStatus variants.
Code evidence: PrivateChatManager.kt; MessageManager.kt; MeshDelegateHandler.kt; MessageComponents.kt; BitchatMessage.kt; AppStateStore.kt
Errors documented: Static inspection found private text messages had no normal Sent/Failed transition. Added Sent/Failed updates around the send callback.
Notes: Needs user-story validation and router failure test.

User story: As a user I want to favorite peers and persist that relationship across sessions.
Expected behavior based on code: MeshPeerListSheet and PrivateChatSheet star buttons call ChatViewModel.toggleFavorite; PrivateChatManager toggles the local fingerprint set, DataManager persists it in SharedPreferences, ChatViewModel updates FavoritesPersistenceService relationship state from Noise keys, and MessageRouter sends favorite/unfavorite notifications via mesh or Nostr when possible.
Code evidence: MeshPeerListSheet.kt; ChatViewModel.kt; PrivateChatManager.kt; DataManager.kt; FavoritesPersistenceService.kt; MessageRouter.kt

User story: As a user I want to block abusive mesh peers and later unblock them.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor exposes /block to list blocked fingerprints or block a nickname and /unblock <nickname>; PrivateChatManager resolves nicknames to peer IDs, stores blocked fingerprints in DataManager, prevents starting/sending private chats with blocked peers, filters incoming blocked sender messages, and emits system messages for block/unblock results.
Errors documented: None found in static inspection; user-facing command result strings remain partly hardcoded and tracked in F089.

User story: As a user I want to see private chat encryption session state before trusting the conversation.
Expected behavior based on code: NoiseSessionIcon maps uninitialized, handshaking, established, and failed states to resource-backed content descriptions, icons, and colors; ChatViewModel keeps peerSessionStates updated from mesh sessions; PrivateChatSheet and SecurityVerificationSheet display session/verification state and allow starting a handshake for eligible peers.

User story: As a user I want to compare fingerprints and mark a peer verified.
Expected behavior based on code: SecurityVerificationSheet displays peer and own fingerprints, session/verified status, long-press copy menu, start-handshake action when no fingerprint is available for a 16-hex peer, mark verified action, and remove verification action; VerificationHandler persists verified fingerprints through SecureIdentityStateManager.

User story: As a user I want to show my QR, scan another QR, paste a verification URL, and complete mutual verification.
Expected behavior based on code: VerificationSheet builds a signed QR URL from VerificationService, renders it as a QR code, scans camera frames with CameraX/ML Kit, now accepts pasted verification URLs with validation feedback, and calls beginQRVerification. VerificationHandler matches the scanned Noise key to connected peers, initiates Noise if needed, sends challenge/response over established sessions, verifies signed responses, persists verified fingerprints, emits system messages/notifications, and MainActivity handles bitchat://verify deep links.
Code evidence: VerificationSheet.kt; VerificationHandler.kt; VerificationService.kt; MainActivity.kt; BluetoothMeshService.kt; MeshCore.kt
Errors documented: Static inspection found the pasted verification URL path was advertised but missing from VerificationSheet. Added paste field, validate action, and invalid URL feedback; tab labels moved to resources.

User story: As a user I want background DMs to notify me and open the relevant private chat.
Expected behavior based on code: NotificationManager shows private notifications when the app is backgrounded or not focused on that sender, groups by sender, includes EXTRA_OPEN_PRIVATE_CHAT and peer extras, and updates summaries. MainActivity handles the extra by showing the peer list/private chat sheet and clearing notifications for that sender; ChatViewModel also clears sender notifications when a private chat starts.

User story: As a user I want geohash messages and mentions to notify me and return to that location channel.
Expected behavior based on code: NotificationManager shows geohash notifications when the app is backgrounded or not focused on that geohash, prioritizes mentions, groups by geohash, includes EXTRA_OPEN_GEOHASH_CHAT, and updates summaries. MainActivity maps geohash length to a channel level, selects the Location channel, updates current notification geohash, and clears that geohash notification; GeohashViewModel clears notifications when switching location channels.

User story: As a user I want mentions in mesh chat to surface when I am away from the timeline.
Expected behavior based on code: MeshDelegateHandler checks public mesh message content for @currentNickname mentions; NotificationManager shows grouped mesh mention notifications when app is backgrounded or user is not viewing mesh, and ChatViewModel/GeohashViewModel clear mesh mention notifications when returning to mesh context.

User story: As a user I want to know when people are nearby if the app is in the background.
Expected behavior based on code: MeshDelegateHandler calls NotificationManager.showActiveUserNotification on peer-list updates; NotificationManager only notifies in background, only for newly seen peers, and only after ACTIVE_PEERS_NOTIFICATION_INTERVAL_MS has elapsed, using resource-backed one/many count text and opening the app on tap.
Notes: Needs notification instrumentation and interval validation.

User story: As a user I want to attach an image, have it compressed, locally echoed, transferred, and shown in the chat.
Expected behavior based on code: ImagePickerButton opens the system image picker on tap and camera capture on long-press with CAMERA permission, downscales selected/captured images into app files, and calls sendImageNote; MediaSendingManager wraps the image as a BitchatFilePacket, adds a local Image message, seeds transfer progress, sends through mesh file APIs, and MessageComponents/ImageMessageItem render the image with progress/cancel support.
Code evidence: ImagePickerButton.kt; ImageUtils.kt; InputComponents.kt; MediaSendingManager.kt; MessageComponents.kt; ImageMessageItem.kt
Notes: Needs UI, camera, and file-system tests.

User story: As a user I want to hold the microphone, see live waveform/timer, release to send, and keep keyboard state stable.
Expected behavior based on code: MessageInput keeps the text field mounted and overlays RealtimeScrollingWaveform/timer during recording; VoiceRecordButton requests RECORD_AUDIO on use, records while pressed, polls amplitude every 80ms, auto-stops at 10 seconds, adds a short release tail, extracts/caches waveform data, and MediaSendingManager sends the audio packet as Audio media.

User story: As a user I want to play/pause received voice notes and tap the waveform to seek.
Expected behavior based on code: AudioMessageItem delegates audio messages to VoiceNotePlayer; VoiceNotePlayer prepares MediaPlayer asynchronously, play/pause toggles playback when prepared, updates progress every 100ms, releases the player on dispose, and passes seek callbacks to WaveformPreview; WaveformViews loads/caches waveform samples and maps tap x-position to a clamped playback fraction.

User story: As a user I want to tap images, browse image messages full-screen, close, and save to Downloads.
Expected behavior based on code: ChatScreen collects image paths from the current timeline and opens FullScreenImageViewer at the tapped image; the viewer uses HorizontalPager, renders unavailable placeholders, shows an image counter, closes via button/dismiss, and saves the current image to MediaStore Downloads with success/failure toasts.

User story: As a user I want to send arbitrary files, not only images and voice notes.
Expected behavior based on code: FilePickerButton copies arbitrary content URIs into app files and calls sendFileNote; MessageInput now exposes FilePickerButton next to ImagePickerButton when media buttons are visible; MediaSendingManager preserves filename/MIME, classifies image/audio/generic file types, adds a local message, seeds transfer progress, and sends through mesh file APIs.
Test status: Static code inspection; Gradle execution blocked
Errors documented: Attachment UI was disabled despite send pipeline existing.
Notes: Needs build/test once Gradle 8.13 is available locally.

User story: As a user I want received files to render as file items and open or save through Android.
Expected behavior based on code: MessageHandler saves incoming file packets and classifies MIME; FileMessageItem renders file metadata, opens FileViewerDialog, saves content to Downloads via MediaStore on Android 10+ with legacy fallback, shows success/failure toasts, and falls back to open-file on save failure; FileViewerDialog now consumes a Boolean save result.
Test status: Static code inspection; Gradle execution blocked
Errors documented: FileMessageItem save callback was placeholder logging only.
Notes: Needs build/test once Gradle 8.13 is available locally.

User story: As a user I want large media sends to show progress and allow cancellation.
Expected behavior based on code: Bluetooth/Mesh file send paths attach stable transfer IDs based on payload hashes; FragmentingPacketSender emits TransferProgressManager start/progress/complete events; ChatViewModel collects events and MediaSendingManager maps transferId to messageId, updates PartiallyDelivered/Delivered status, and cancelMediaSend asks mesh to cancel, removes the local cached file when found, removes the message, and clears transfer maps.
Code evidence: BluetoothMeshService.kt; MeshCore.kt; FragmentingPacketSender.kt; TransferProgressManager.kt; MediaSendingManager.kt; MessageComponents.kt
Notes: Needs fragmentation test with fake transport.

User story: As a user I want /join or /j to join an existing channel or create a new one.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor handles /j and /join, normalizes the # prefix, accepts an optional password, and calls ChannelManager.joinChannel; ChannelManager verifies or prompts for passwords when needed, adds new joined channel state, sets creator for non-protected new channels, adds self as member, initializes channel messages, switches current channel, saves channel data/key commitments, and emits a joined system message on success.

User story: As a user I want to leave a joined channel from the header or sidebar.
Expected behavior based on code: ChatHeader channel leave action calls ChatViewModel.leaveChannel; ChannelManager removes the channel from joined state, exits it if selected, removes messages/unread, members, keys/passwords/key commitment, and creator, then persists data. ChatViewModel sends a public mesh left-channel message after leaving.

User story: As a user I want to switch between joined channels and see unread activity.
Expected behavior based on code: ChannelManager.switchToChannel sets currentChannel, exits private-chat selection, and clears unread for the selected channel; MessageManager increments unread counts for classic or geohash channel messages only when not currently viewing that channel; MeshPeerListSheet shows unread counts for channel rows and ChatHeader shows joined channel count.

User story: As a channel creator I want to set a password and have protected channels prompt for a password when joining.
Expected behavior based on code: /pass checks current channel and creator, stores a derived key and persisted local key commitment, marks channel protected, and password-protected joins can derive a local key from the supplied password. Creators without a loaded key are prompted instead of bypassing password entry.
Errors documented: The unconditional password-verification stub was replaced. Persisted local commitments reject wrong passwords after reload; remote/shared commitments are still not distributed, so passwords for externally protected channels without a local commitment are validated only by successful decryption of future messages.
Retest status: Blocked: Gradle wrapper download denied by sandbox
Notes: Added tests for password join key derivation and persisted commitment rejection; cross-peer commitment sharing remains a protocol gap.

User story: As a user in a password-protected channel I want channel messages encrypted on the wire.
Expected behavior based on code: Protected channel sends now AES-GCM encrypt the message, send it in a BitchatMessage binary envelope, and receivers decode/decrypt that envelope before plaintext fallback. Structured encrypted payloads that cannot decrypt are dropped instead of rendered as plaintext.
Code evidence: ChatViewModel.kt; ChannelManager.kt; MeshService.kt; BluetoothMeshService.kt; MeshCore.kt; MessageHandler.kt
Errors documented: The no-op send path was replaced. Protected sends without a loaded key are blocked rather than falling back to plaintext, and undecryptable protected payloads no longer fall through to plaintext rendering.
Retest status: Blocked: Gradle wrapper download denied by sandbox
Notes: Added tests for encrypted payload creation/decryption and binary envelope preservation.

User story: As a channel owner/user I want /save to toggle local channel message retention if that feature is implemented.
Expected behavior based on code: /save is not implemented in processCommand and MessageRetentionService remains commented out; README and autocomplete no longer advertise it as an available command.
Test status: Blocked: Gradle wrapper download denied by sandbox
Errors documented: False /save README and autocomplete claims were present.
Fix status: UX false advertising fixed; feature implementation deferred
Notes: CommandProcessorTest now includes coverage for autocomplete not advertising /save.

User story: As a channel owner I want /transfer to transfer ownership if that feature is implemented.
Expected behavior based on code: /transfer is not implemented in processCommand and ChannelManager has no transfer ownership method; README and autocomplete no longer advertise it as an available command.
Test status: Blocked: Gradle wrapper download denied by sandbox
Errors documented: False /transfer README and autocomplete claims were present.
Fix status: UX false advertising fixed; feature implementation deferred
Notes: CommandProcessorTest now includes coverage for autocomplete not advertising /transfer.

User story: As a user I want typing / to suggest valid commands for the current context.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor filters base commands and implemented channel-only commands by prefix/aliases and ChatInputSection displays CommandSuggestionsBox.
Test status: Blocked: Gradle wrapper download denied by sandbox
Errors documented: Autocomplete included /save and /transfer even though execution was missing.

User story: As a user I want @mentions to suggest available peers or geohash participants.
Expected behavior based on code: ChatScreen updates mention suggestions on input changes and inserts selected suggestions into the TextFieldValue; CommandProcessor shows suggestions after the last @ only when no space follows it, uses mesh peer nicknames in mesh context or geohashPeople display names in location context, excludes self, filters by prefix case-insensitively, and replaces text from the last @ through the end with @nickname plus a trailing space.

User story: As a user I want /w to list online mesh users or geohash participants based on context.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor.handleWhoCommand builds a mesh online-users list from connected peer nicknames in mesh context, or a geohash participant list from geohashPeople excluding self in location context, then posts a system message with either the context label and names or no one else is around right now.
Errors documented: None found in static inspection; user-facing command strings remain hardcoded and tracked in F089.

User story: As a user I want /clear to clear the currently visible conversation context.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor.handleClearCommand checks selected private chat first and clears that peer conversation, else clears current channel messages when in a classic channel, else clears main messages and all channel messages through MessageManager.clearMessages.

User story: As a user I want /channels to list joined channels.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor.handleChannelsCommand reads ChannelManager.getJoinedChannelsList, posts no channels joined when empty, otherwise posts joined channels with the sorted channel list as a system message.
Errors documented: None found in static inspection; user-facing command strings remain hardcoded and tracked in F089.

User story: As a user I want /msg to open a private chat and optionally send an immediate message.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor.handleMessageCommand resolves nickname to peer ID, starts PrivateChatManager, posts a started-chat system message when no body is supplied, and when content is supplied creates/sends a private message through PrivateChatManager and MessageRouter-backed routing when a ChatViewModel is available.

User story: As a user I want /block with no argument to list blocked users or with a nickname to block that peer.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor strips a leading @ and delegates block-by-nickname; with no argument it posts PrivateChatManager.listBlockedUsers.
Notes: Command result text is hardcoded and tracked under F089; needs fake MeshService test.

User story: As a user I want /unblock nickname to allow messages from that peer again.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor strips a leading @, delegates unblock-by-nickname, and posts usage when no target is provided.
Notes: Command result text is hardcoded and tracked under F089; needs fake MeshService test.

User story: As a user I want playful action commands to send formatted action messages in mesh, channel, private, or geohash context.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor builds a formatted action message, routes it through private/geohash/channel/public send paths, and ChatUserSheet triggers /slap and /hug actions.

User story: As a user I want typo commands to explain that the command is unknown.
Expected behavior based on code: CommandProcessor posts a system message for unknown commands and suggests typing / for available commands.
Notes: Existing tests are ignored; should re-enable with fake MeshService.

User story: As a user I want location channel UI to explain permission state and offer settings if permission is denied.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationChannelsSheet now distinguishes app toggle, Android system location, and runtime permission: system-off shows a location-settings prompt; effective-enabled shows denied/settings or granted permission text.
Test status: Static code inspection; Gradle execution blocked
Errors documented: System location off with app toggle on previously hid the real blocker and reused the enable button state.
Fix status: Prompt added; app/system location states separated in UI
Retest status: Static retest passed; Gradle wrapper download denied by sandbox

User story: As a user I want to switch between Bluetooth mesh and Nostr geohash location channels.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationChannelsSheet selects ChannelID.Mesh or ChannelID.Location; LocationChannelManager persists the selection and GeohashViewModel switches subscriptions and current geohash state.
Notes: Needs fake manager test or instrumentation.

Feature: Nearby channel list and participant counts
User story: As a user I want nearby channels grouped by precision with names, coverage, and people counts.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationChannelsSheet lists available channels excluding building level, shows coverage/name, highlights channels with participants, and uses ? people for high-precision zero counts; Nostr presence/chat events update reactive counts.

User story: As a user I want to bookmark and revisit geohashes.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationChannelsSheet and ChatHeader toggle bookmarks; GeohashBookmarksStore normalizes, de-duplicates, persists bookmarks, and resolves friendly names.

Feature: Custom geohash teleport and map picker
User story: As a user I want to type or select a custom geohash from a map and teleport to it.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationChannelsSheet filters custom input to valid geohash base32, accepts up to 12 chars, opens GeohashPickerActivity, and marks manual custom selections teleported before selecting the channel.

User story: As a user I want to disable or enable location channels from the location sheet.
Expected behavior based on code: LocationChannelsSheet button now reflects the app-level location preference while nearby lists/live refresh remain gated by effective app-and-system state; LocationChannelManager persists the app toggle and clears location channels when disabled.

User story: As a user I want messages in location channels to go through Nostr relays with my geohash identity.
Expected behavior based on code: ChatViewModel routes normal and command-generated public sends to GeohashViewModel when selected channel is Location; GeohashViewModel adds local geo timeline echo, derives geohash identity, creates kind 20000 event with nickname/teleport tags, and sends to nearest geohash relays.
Notes: Requires network or mocked relay tests.

User story: As a user I want geohash messages and participant list to update from Nostr events.
Expected behavior based on code: GeohashViewModel subscribes to geohash events and geohash gift wraps; GeohashMessageHandler validates kind/geohash/PoW, drops blocked users, updates participants/nicknames/teleport state, ignores own events, and adds remote chat messages.

User story: As a user I want participant counts to reflect nearby online users while preserving privacy.
Expected behavior based on code: GeohashViewModel observes available channels, broadcasts kind 20001 presence only to precision <=5 geohashes, uses randomized loop/per-broadcast delays, and restarts when the channel set changes.
Notes: Needs timing/privacy tests with coroutine test scheduler.

User story: As a user I want to see people in a geohash and start a DM from their pubkey or nickname.
Expected behavior based on code: GeohashPeopleList sorts current user first and latest activity after, shows unread/teleport states, and starts a geohash DM for other people; GeohashViewModel maps nostr_ conversation keys to pubkeys/source geohash and opens the private chat.

Fix status: Removed misleading TODO comment; behavior remains enabled

User story: As a user I want to block an abusive geohash user by nickname.
